evals.prompt: REPLACE: A copy brief with target audience, offer, medium (email/ad/landing page), word count range, and any specific constraints
evals.expected_output: REPLACE: Description of ideal copy output including framework, tone, and structure

assertions.text: Copy uses the specified framework (PAS, AIDA, or Proof Stack) with clear section transitions
Headline is under 12 words and leads with a benefit, curiosity gap, or pain point

Body copy contains no banned AI words (delve, tapestry, realm, landscape, ever-evolving, cutting-edge, robust, transformative, pivotal, vibrant, crucial, compelling, seamless, groundbreaking, leverage, harness, embark, navigate, unveil, facilitate, synergy, game-changer, unlock, unleash, elevate, utilize, endeavour, multifaceted)
At least one specific number, stat, or data point appears in the first 50 words
CTA is present, uses an action verb, and matches the funnel stage (ToF=engage, MoF=learn, BoF=buy)
Copy addresses the reader directly using 'you' or 'your', not 'we' or 'our' in the body
No em dashes (U+2014 '—') are used anywhere — hyphens (-) or en dashes (–) only
Total word count is within the specified range for the copy type
Risk reversal, guarantee, or objection handling is present
Urgency or scarcity element is genuine and specific (deadline, limited spots, real constraint) — not fabricated hype

evals.prompt: REPLACE: A second copy brief for a different medium or funnel stage to test versatility

assertions.text: Copy uses the correct framework for the specified funnel stage
Headline creates curiosity or highlights a specific pain point
No banned AI words or phrases are used
Opening line hooks attention within the first 10 words
CTA is clear, single, and action-oriented
Copy tone matches the specified audience (casual/professional/urgent)
No em dashes used
Specific proof elements (testimonials, stats, case studies) are included
The copy creates a logical flow from problem to solution to action
No filler sentences that could be removed without losing meaning
